author: David Huu Pham
text:
REVIEWER 2 (all axes) cycle 3: REVISE

Findings: 1 BLOCKER / 0 IMPORTANT / 0 MINOR
bestiary-dl3z UnknownSuffixOverflow positive test: SHAM
bestiary-afda MIGRATION fix: fixed

Rationale: The positive test (UnknownSuffixOverflow_PositiveCase) is structurally regression-blind. The test input rawFamily='claude-opus-4-1-extra-stuff-zen' triggers ParseFamilyWithVersion Step 5 fallback, which returns family=full_string (all 7 tokens absorbed into family). detectSuffixOverflow then computes knownTokens from that full family string, leaving extra=0 — the overflow threshold of >2 is never met, so ReasonUnknownSuffixOverflow never fires. The test then hits the t.Logf('no failure emitted...') branch and returns without calling t.Error or t.Fatal: it PASSES unconditionally regardless of whether the code path under test is functional or completely deleted. A correct positive test must use an input where ParseFamilyWithVersion returns a short canonical family (e.g. via override lookup) so that extra tokens remain unaccounted-for by detectSuffixOverflow; the assertion must be t.Errorf (not t.Logf) when failure==nil. The MIGRATION fix (bestiary-afda) is confirmed clean: grep for bare ReasonSuffixOverflow returns 0 hits; both renamed constants appear correctly at lines 904-905 and 1087-1099.

author: David Huu Pham
text: Design choice for Fix 1 visual marker: '*' suffix appended to the canonical entry in the Canonical column (e.g. 'anthropic/claude/opus/4@2025-05-14 *'). Rationale: (1) Most surgical — extends existing ambiguousCandidateRow format string and existing for-loop without rebuilding anything. A 4th 'canonical' column would require changing the format string width spec and the header/separator rows. (2) Compact and legible — '*' is a universally understood 'primary' marker, adds only 2 chars, fits within the 40-char column with no overflow for typical canonical forms. (3) Consistent with CLI conventions — footnote markers like '*' are idiomatic in tabular CLIs (e.g. git branch, apt list). (4) Sorting canonical rows to top (before N=10 truncation) ensures they always appear even with many rehosts, and the '*' makes them visually distinct in position and appearance.

REVIEWER AXIS A (CORRECTNESS) — VOTE: REVISE

**Verdict:** REVISE — one BLOCKER correctness defect found.

**AC observations run:**
- Build: CGO_ENABLED=0 go build ./cmd/bestiary — PASS
- Tests: CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./... -count=1 — PASS (all 3 packages)
- Vet: go vet ./... — PASS
- AC1 (bestiary show claude): legend present, 5 *-prefixed canonical rows, '+9 more', 5 rehost names + '+24 more', footer — PASS
- AC2 (no-rehost omits Section 2): TestFormatAmbiguous_V4_RehostSection_AbsentWhenEmpty PASS + confirmed by test run
- AC3 (* is PREFIX not suffix): confirmed '* anthropic/...' lines in output — PASS
- AC4 (PURL note): 'no matches in namespace "nonexistent"' prints above Canonical: — PASS
- AC5 (no regression): claude/opus@2025-11-24 -> Provider=anthropic; pkg:huggingface/anthropic/claude-opus-4-5 -> Provider=anthropic — PASS
- AC6: go test ./... + go vet ./... — PASS

**BLOCKER (bestiary-ylb8):** resolve.go:161-172 — PURL loose-fallback candidateMap deduplicates by model ID (first-seen wins), NOT by canonical-provider preference. For 'pkg:huggingface/nonexistent/claude-opus-4-5', azure-cognitive-services appears before anthropic in staticModels, so candidates[0].Provider = azure-cognitive-services. FormatAmbiguous Section 1 filters by Provider==CanonicalProvider() — azure != anthropic — Section 1 is EMPTY. Anthropic is ALSO excluded from Section 2 (it IS canonical). The canonical provider disappears entirely from both sections. Confirmed by running: './bestiary show --format purl pkg:huggingface/nonexistent/claude-opus-4-5'. This violates UAT-5 Q4 ('show 5 canonical provider models'). The multi-group case already has the correct fix (resolve.go:267-276: canonical-provider preference in rep selection). The PURL loose-fallback candidateMap is missing the same logic.

author: David Huu Pham
text:
FINDING [IMPORTANT-1]: Footer does not address 'see all rehosts for a model' — misleading gap.

Location: format.go FormatAmbiguous() footer section (lines 406-407).

Issue: The user's verbatim UAT-5 Q4 requested 'Instructions should also given to see all rehosts, OR all providers/variants in general.' The footer addresses (b) all providers/variants via 'bestiary list' but does NOT address (a) see all rehosts for a specific model. The line 'To resolve an exact model ID: bestiary show <raw-id> --format=raw' runs a valid command but returns ONE model (most-recent-wins single provider), not all providers hosting that model ID. It is labelled as 'resolve exact model ID' not 'see all rehosts' — so the label is accurate but the gap in user guidance remains: no instruction exists to show all providers hosting a given raw ID.

End-user impact: A user who wants to know 'who else hosts claude-3-5-haiku-20241022?' will not find a clear path from the disambiguation output. The footer partially addresses the user request but leaves the 'see all rehosts' use case unaddressed.

Why severity IMPORTANT not BLOCKER: The user's Q4 phrased this as 'see all rehosts, OR all providers/variants in general' — the OR gives a partial pass since 'bestiary list' does satisfy 'all providers/variants in general'. The footer commands are real and verified. This is a gap in helpfulness, not a broken/misleading command. No current CLI command can filter by model ID across all providers, so the fix requires either: (a) a new flag like 'bestiary list --id <raw-id>', or (b) a note like 'or: bestiary list 2>/dev/null | grep <raw-id>' in the footer, or (c) accepting this as a known gap documented in a follow-up.

Suggested fix: Add a note in the footer such as 'To see all providers for a specific model ID: bestiary list | grep <raw-id>' or open a follow-up for a --id filter on list.

author: David Huu Pham
text:
FINDING [IMPORTANT-2]: Empty 'Canonical:' section with no rows is confusing UX for unmapped families.

Location: format.go FormatAmbiguous() Section 1 rendering (lines 370-382).

Observed: Running 'bestiary show minimax' produces:

  bestiary: input "minimax" matched multiple canonicals

  * = canonical provider

  Canonical:

  Also rehosted by:
  minimax, deepinfra, ...

The empty 'Canonical:' header with zero rows is presented to the user without explanation. A user has no way to know why Canonical is empty — is it a bug? No canonical exists? The family is unmapped?

End-user impact: Confusing/broken-looking output for any family where CanonicalProvider() returns ''. With 45+ unmapped families (everything outside claude/gemini/gpt/o/llama/mistral/codestral/devstral/deepseek/qwen), this is a real and common scenario.

Why IMPORTANT not BLOCKER: The spec explicitly marked unmapped-family coverage as OUT OF SCOPE (bestiary-1wy7 followup). The test suite does not cover this case. The correct fix requires parser/family mapping changes. However the output is not actively misleading (it doesn't show wrong data), just sparse and confusing.

Severity reasoning (from reviewer brief): 'judge severity (likely IMPORTANT/MINOR → FOLLOWUP, unless actively misleading)'. Classifying as IMPORTANT because it appears for a large class of common queries (any unmapped family), not just an edge case.

Suggested fix for a follow-up: Either (a) suppress the 'Canonical:' section header when canonicalRows is empty (just show the rehost section with a note like '(no canonical provider mapped)'), or (b) add a conditional note: 'Note: no canonical provider is known for this family'.

author: David Huu Pham
text:
FINDING [MINOR-1]: '+N more' truncation hints lack section-level disambiguation.

Location: format.go FormatAmbiguous() lines 380, 396-397.

Issue: The truncation hints '+9 more' (canonical) and '+24 more' (rehosts) are bare counters without labels indicating what was truncated. While section headers provide some context, a user skimming the output might not associate '+9 more' with 'canonical models' vs '+24 more' with 'rehost providers'. Compare to the previous implementation which said '+M more (use --format=raw with a specific model ID to see all)'.

End-user impact: Minor readability concern — users may not immediately know what they're missing in each section.

Suggested fix: Optional enhancement such as '+9 more canonical variants' / '+24 more rehost providers', or simply keep as-is since section headers make context clear. Very low priority.

author: David Huu Pham
text:
FINDING [MINOR-2]: Rehost ordering is first-seen registry order (not alphabetical/sorted).

Location: resolve.go collectRehostProviders() lines 291-305; format.go Section 2 rendering.

Issue: The 5 rehost providers displayed are the first 5 seen in registry order. For 'bestiary show claude', the output shows 'deepinfra, perplexity-agent, azure-cognitive-services, fastrouter, nano-gpt' which is deterministic (stable across runs, confirmed) but not particularly meaningful to the user — it doesn't prioritize well-known/popular providers.

End-user impact: Minor UX — alphabetical sorting or popularity-weighted ordering would be more predictable and useful. However first-seen stable order is not confusing, just suboptimal.

Suggested fix: Sort RehostProviders alphabetically before rendering. This would be a follow-up enhancement.

author: David Huu Pham

- * is a PREFIX ✓

- Up to 5 distinct rehost NAMES in Section 2 ✓

- PURL Fix 2 note unchanged ✓
- No-rehost case handled (confirmed via TestFormatAmbiguous_V4_RehostSection_AbsentWhenEmpty) ✓

## Findings

BLOCKER: None. bestiary-rr3o closed (empty).

IMPORTANT (2 items — follow-up):
- [IMPORTANT-1] Footer gap: no instruction covers 'see all rehosts for a specific model'. The user's Q4 used 'OR' so the footer's 'bestiary list' partial pass avoids BLOCKER. Tracked in bestiary-nhlh.
- [IMPORTANT-2] Empty 'Canonical:' section for unmapped families (e.g., minimax) renders a bare header with no rows — confusing but out-of-scope per bestiary-1wy7. Tracked in bestiary-nhlh.

MINOR (2 items — cosmetic):
- [MINOR-1] '+N more' hints are bare (no section-level label). Low impact given section headers. Tracked in bestiary-mghy.
- [MINOR-2] Rehost provider order is first-seen (not sorted). Stable across runs. Tracked in bestiary-mghy.

No BLOCKERs found. Implementation delivers the core UAT-5 Q4 requirements faithfully. IMPORTANT items are pre-existing architecture gaps (bestiary-1wy7) or missing CLI features (list --id) appropriate for a follow-up, not a re-roll of this slice.

author: David Huu Pham
text:
Classification (Phase 1 s1_1):
- scope=narrow (single package cmd/bestiary-gen; deterministic ordering only)
- complexity=low (sort + stable collision assignment + reproducibility test + one-time regen)
- risk=low-behavioral / high-diff-volume (no model-set or field changes; but the canonical regen produces a large one-time diff that must be verified as semantically-empty)

author: David Huu Pham
text:
Explore (Phase 1 s1_3, light — minimal research per user):
Consumers of the models []ModelInfo slice in cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go (all must see a stable order):
- fetchModelsWithRaw (~584-664): assembles the slice via append in provider/model iteration order (non-deterministic source).
- generateSource (~903): emits staticModels = []ModelInfo{...} in slice order → struct ordering.
- generateConstantsSource (~1655) → resolveCollisions (~1478): assigns _N collision suffix in slice order → unstable suffix→ID mapping. NOTE: constants are then sorted by constName at emit (~1680), so NAMES are stable but the _N→ID binding is not.
- collectFamilies (~671): already sorts internally (sorted output) — unaffected by slice order, but verify.
- countUniqueProviders (~1063): count only — order-independent.
Single-point fix: sort models by (Provider, ID) once after assembly in fetchModelsWithRaw. Confirm resolveCollisions has no internal map iteration that re-introduces nondeterminism. No domain research needed (Go reproducible-codegen = sort before emit; gofmt already deterministic).

author: David Huu Pham
text:
Classification (Phase 1 s1_1):
- scope=moderate (parse.go core + parse/data/*.json overrides + codegen wiring + regen; provenance-adjacent only via release timing)
- complexity=moderate-high (multiple distinct heuristic classes + override curation across 121 cases; rc1/rc2 staging)
- risk=behavioral-output-change (alters decomposition/canonical forms for ~121+ models; must NOT regress the 636 already-correct version extractions or the 208 modifier populations)
- novelty=low-moderate (parsing heuristics known; canonical-form judgment for irregular IDs needs care)
- sequencing: planning parallel with Epoch 1; implementation AFTER v0.2.1 lands.

author: David Huu Pham
text:
Explore (parallel planning) complete — key findings for proposal:
- ALL 121 parse failures are a SINGLE class: ReasonVersionDigitsNotExtracted (version digits between family-prefix and variant). Buckets: gpt-mini(20), gemini-pro(17), gpt-nano(17), gemini-flash(16), claude-sonnet(12), claude-haiku(10), gpt-pro(10), gpt-codex(2). Pattern: <family>-<version-digits>-<variant>[-suffix] where rawFamily (e.g. 'gpt-mini') doesn't align with the ID path ('gpt-5-mini').
- Mistral YYMM (ReasonYYMMDateAsVersion) and UnknownSuffixOverflow classes are currently EMPTY in the real data (0 failures) — detectors exist but don't fire. So rc1's systematic fix is essentially this ONE Mode-1 class. (bestiary-eq7w YYMM + bestiary-e9pi overflow-reachability are latent, not active.)
- Pipeline (parse.go): ParseFamily→ParseFamilyWithVersion (override→version_patterns→suffix→fallback); ExtractVersionFromID (ID-based, when family yields empty version); ExtractModifier; InferFamilyFromIDWithVariant (empty-RawFamily path). Codegen: genToModelInfoDetailed wires them; non-empty-family path emits ParseFailure via ParseFamilyDetailed, empty-family path emits NONE.
- Blast radius (must not regress): ~527 Version-populated, ~208 Modifier-populated, ~3802 Variant-populated, 100% Family. Authoritative test tables: TestParseFamily_Overrides (70+), TestParseFamily_SuffixStripping (30). Determinism test (100-iter) exists.
- Interaction w/ Epoch 1: parse_failures.json already sorted by provider/raw_id; Epoch 1 determinism makes the regen diff legible for Epoch 2's parser changes. Full map: explore agent output (in transcript).

author: David Huu Pham
text:
ARCHITECT EMPIRICAL PROVENANCE (Phase 3, 2026-05-30): All findings below verified by running 'go run ./cmd/bestiary-gen --no-fetch' twice against committed .bestiary-gen-cache/api_response.json, then restoring committed gen files (working tree clean).

(1) Nondeterminism confirmed: regen1 vs regen2 swap _1/_2 binding on the SAME 24 collision groups every run; models_static_gen.go = 74384 line churn; counts unchanged (4329 models/115 providers/169 families/121 parse failures) -> R6 holds.

(2) TRUE collision surface = 24 groups / 48 constants (NOT the seed bug's approximate ~28; bestiary-9lnq said ~28 suffix swaps but exact count of bound constants is 48 across 24 groups). Every group is 2 members of the SAME model differing only by raw-ID path-prefix or punctuation.

(3) The 1 IRREDUCIBLE group: Model__CloudflareAIGateway__Claude__3__5__Haiku — anthropic/claude-3.5-haiku vs anthropic/claude-3-5-haiku. Identical prefix; the only distinguisher is '.' vs '-', which the tokenizer drops, so BOTH normalize to the same fragment AND the same full-id PascalCase. This forces every scheme to keep a deterministic ordinal fallback (raw-ID-sorted: '-' 0x2D < '.' 0x2E).

(4) Casing of candidate prefix disambiguators (emulating existing tokenToConstPart + casingOverrides): tee->Tee, pro->Pro, siliconflow->Siliconflow, openai->Openai, deepseek-ai->DeepseekAI, zai-org->ZaiOrg, kilo-auto->KiloAuto, z-ai->ZAI (ai override), moonshotai->Moonshotai, xiaomi->Xiaomi, qwen->Qwen, kimi->Kimi, bare->Bare(sentinel).

(5) Root-cause precision: the two range-over-map loops in resolveCollisions (~L1492 nameToPositions, ~L1557 finalSeen) are order-SAFE because they only write result[pos] by position; the SOLE nondeterminism is the unsorted models slice feeding positions. R1 stabilizes positions; R2 additionally removes position-dependence (raw-ID-keyed ordinal).

(6) R4 BLOCKER: .bestiary-gen-cache/api_response.json is UNTRACKED by git (git ls-files empty). Up-to-date guard must use a committed hermetic testdata fixture, not the live cache. apiURL is a var (L156) -> overridable for httptest.

(7) AGENTS.md confirmed real file; CLAUDE.md+GEMINI.md are symlinks to it (R5 single edit point).

author: David Huu Pham
text:
PLAN-REVIEW-B: REVISE

Axis: test quality (R3 reproducibility unit test, R4 up-to-date guard, existing test update, determinism regression guard).

---

## BLOCKER-1: Reproducibility fixture does not exercise the R2 code path

The committed fixture at cmd/bestiary-gen/testdata/reproducibility_fixture.json (1402 bytes) contains only 7 models across 3 providers. Its two 'mystery' models — 'claude-mystery' and 'claude-mystery-v2' — collide on a base name but are distinguished by the version-suffix path (pass a, extractVersionSegment extracts 'v2'). They will never hit the sequential/positional fallback that R2 replaces.

The fixture contains NO prefixed raw IDs (no '/' in any model ID), so it cannot exercise the prefix-fragment disambiguator at all. The R3 test as designed (call pipeline twice, assert bytes.Equal) would pass trivially even with a correct R2 implementation, because the fixture only exercises pass (a), which has always been deterministic for version-distinct colliders.

This is a sham-test risk: the reproducibility test may be green forever regardless of whether R2 is implemented correctly or at all, because the fixture never routes any model into the R2 code branch.

Fix: add at least one collision group to the fixture whose members have identical version segments but different path prefixes — for example, two models with the same base ID ('free' or 'deepseek-v3') appearing under different namespace prefixes ('openrouter/free' and 'kilo-auto/free' style). This forces the fixture through the R2 prefix-fragment disambiguator. Also include the punctuation-only group (two models identical except '.' vs '-') to exercise the raw-ID-sorted ordinal fallback, since that is the one path in Scheme (c) that cannot be verified otherwise.

---

## BLOCKER-2: TestResolveCollisions_SequentialSuffix must be updated but the plan is non-binding

The plan's Validation Checklist item says "Existing TestResolveCollisions_SequentialSuffix updated to assert MEANINGFUL suffix (not bare _N)". The existing test at cmd/bestiary-gen/main_test.go ~L421 explicitly asserts strings.Contains(resolved[0], "_1") and strings.Contains(resolved[0], "_2") — it requires bare numeric suffixes. After R2 lands, this test must either be updated to assert the new prefix-fragment suffix format, or kept as-is by adjusting the test input to use an irreducible punctuation-only collision (the one case where Scheme (c) legitimately emits a raw-ID-sorted ordinal, which would still produce '_1'/'_2').

The plan mentions this in the checklist but does not prescribe exactly which path the test takes or what the new assertion looks like. If the implementer leaves the old assertion in place, the test becomes a false negative (it passes, but only because the implementer forgot to update it and the failing R2 code happens to still produce '_1'/'_2' for the test's inputs). This is a sham-test risk in the opposite direction.

Fix: the plan must commit to one of: (a) update the test input to be a genuine punctuation-only collision and keep the '_1'/'_2' assertion as correct, or (b) update the test to assert the new prefix-fragment format and document what the expected fragment is for the test's specific models. Either is acceptable; the plan must specify which.

---

## IMPORTANT-1: R4 fixture is a different artifact from the R3 fixture — plan conflates them

The plan discusses the R4 up-to-date guard as needing a 'committed hermetic testdata fixture', and the R3 reproducibility test as needing 'a fixed input'. A single fixture can serve both only if it is rich enough for both purposes. The current fixture satisfies neither (per BLOCKER-1). The plan does not clarify whether the same testdata/api_response.golden.json serves both R3 and R4, or whether they use separate files.

If R4 uses a 'golden' fixture plus committed golden gen-file snippets, those snippets must be small (not the full 1.8 MB output). The plan recommends small curated fixtures but does not specify what 'committed golden gen.go snippets' means in practice — whether that is a full committed gen file (wrong, changes every upstream sync) or a committed expected-output buffer kept in testdata (correct, but need to specify). Clarify the R4 artifact design: what files are committed, what does the guard read and compare, and how those files are updated when R2 scheme output changes.

---

## IMPORTANT-2: go/format version sensitivity is a real flakiness risk for R4

Both generateSource and generateConstantsSource pass their output through format.Source (go/format). The go/format output is stable for a fixed Go toolchain version, but it can change between Go minor versions. The R4 guard compares regenerated bytes against committed golden outputs. If the committed goldens were produced with Go 1.24 and CI runs with Go 1.25+, the guard will fail spuriously.

The plan does not address this. Two mitigations exist: (1) the guard strips/ignores the generated header comment (which contains a timestamp or tool path), or (2) the guard normalizes both sides through go/format before comparing (eliminating formatting differences). The plan should specify which approach is taken. For correctness, option (2) — normalize both sides — is stronger because it tests semantic equivalence rather than byte identity on the committed artifact.

---

## MINOR-1: N=5 iterations for R3 is stated but not justified as sufficient

The existing TestParseFamily_Determinism uses 100 iterations. The proposal states N=5 for R3. For map-iteration nondeterminism in Go (where map ordering is deliberately randomized per-run), 5 iterations has approximately a (5/N_map_orderings)^k failure probability, which may be low enough to miss a bug on most runs. The parse determinism precedent in this codebase is 100 iterations. The proposal should either justify why 5 suffices (e.g., because R1's sort makes all map paths irrelevant and the only randomness left is provider-count-scale) or adopt the codebase's established 100-iteration convention.

---

## MINOR-2: Fixture missing raw_id fields for prefix-path collision models

When the fixture is extended per BLOCKER-1, the models representing prefix-path collisions must have raw IDs that include the '/' separator (e.g. 'openrouter/free' and 'kilo-auto/free'). The models.dev wire format uses 'id' as the key within each provider's model map. Confirm whether the generator reads the prefixed form from the wire 'id' field or constructs it differently, so the fixture accurately represents what the live API returns for these collision cases. This should be verified empirically before the fixture is committed.

---

## Summary

The fixture as committed does not exercise the R2 disambiguation path. Both R3 and the updated TestResolveCollisions_SequentialSuffix risk being false-green unless the plan specifies exactly which collision shapes the tests must cover and how the existing test will be updated. The go/format version sensitivity for R4 is an unaddressed flakiness risk. These are the primary concerns.

Vote: REVISE

author: David Huu Pham
text:
PLAN-REVIEW-C: Design & Public-API Impact — REVISE

VOTE: REVISE

## Summary

The R1 foundation (sort by (Provider,ID)) and R3/R4/R5/R7 plan are sound and well-specified. The core issue is that the R2 section contains a material factual error in its scope count, which cascades into understated tradeoff analysis and potentially an incomplete scheme design. This must be corrected before the user chooses a scheme at Plan UAT.

---

## Finding 1 — IMPORTANT: R2 collision-surface count is materially wrong (24 groups stated; actual count is 57)

The proposal states 'True collision surface = 24 groups / 48 constants' and the architect's comment corroborates '24 collision groups, 48 constants'. However, the committed models_constants_gen.go (regenerated 2026-05-30, commit c491e7b) contains **57 distinct collision groups** with _1/_2 suffix pairs, covering 114+ affected constants. Running:

  python3 — count_collision_pairs.py (see evidence below)

yields 57 groups, not 24. This is a >2x undercount.

**What the discrepancy means:** The architect empirically measured '18 changed lines per regen' — that is ~9 unstable groups (those whose _N binding flips between runs). The '24' figure appears to be the architect's broader estimate of groups that fall through to the bare sequential fallback. But the committed file shows 57 groups with _1/_2 suffixes, including several new categories the proposal does not cover:

- **Version-only pairs within the same provider (no prefix):** e.g.  vs  (both bare IDs, no slash) collide at  because the version-suffix pass (a) apparently does not disambiguate them. Affected: 302AI, OpenAI, Azure, AIHubMix, Abacus, AzureCognitiveServices, GitHubCopilot, Jiekou, Kilo, Llmgateway, OpenCode, ZenMux, and others — roughly 15-20 additional groups.
- **Same-prefix pairs where prefix exists but is identical:** e.g.  vs  — both have  prefix giving the SAME fragment . The disambiguation scheme (a) collapses to the same fragment. These are CloudflareAIGateway, NanoGPT, OpenRouter, PerplexityAgent, Requesty groups.
- **NanoGPT TEE/* vs other-prefix for many new families:** Glm__4__6, Glm__4__7, Glm__4__7__Flash, Glm__5, GPT__Oss__120b, GPT__Oss__20b, Kimi__K2__5, Kimi__K2__5__Thinking, Kimi__K2__Thinking, Minimax__M2__1, Qwen3__5__397b__A17b — 11 more groups beyond the 4 NanoGPT groups mentioned in the proposal.
- **duo-chat-gpt-5-1 vs duo-chat-gpt-5-2 (GitLab):** different IDs, no version field, no prefix — the only distinguisher is trailing digit.

**Impact on schemes:** Scheme (a)'s uniqueness claim '23/24 groups outright' is incorrect — many of the gpt-5.1/5.2 groups and same-prefix pairs would ALSO fail scheme (a), leaving a larger ordinal-fallback residue than described. The irreducible set may be significantly larger than 1 group.

**Required fix:** Re-run the collision surface analysis against the committed cache (or the actual generated file) and restate: (1) total groups, (2) how many are disambiguable by prefix-fragment (a), (3) how many need full-ID (b), (4) how many remain for ordinal fallback.

---

## Finding 2 — IMPORTANT: Scheme (a) and (c) worked examples use a provider-specific base (Model__NanoGPT__Deepseek__V3__1) that is NOT 'the' worked example — it does NOT represent the gpt-5.x / same-prefix classes

The worked examples in the proposal ('NanoGPT Deepseek V3.1', 'Kilo Free', 'AlibabaCN Kimi K2.5', 'SiliconFlowCN Deepseek V3') are all correct for their specific cases. The prefix-fragment fragments I traced are accurate:
- deepseek-ai → DeepseekAI ✓, TEE → Tee ✓, kilo-auto → KiloAuto ✓, kimi → Kimi ✓, bare → Bare ✓, Pro/deepseek-ai → ProDeepseekAI ✓

But the worked examples only cover the 'TEE/* vs namespace/*' and 'bare vs prefixed' shapes. There is no worked example showing how scheme (a) or (c) handles the gpt-5.1/5.2 shape (where both IDs have the same prefix, different version only) or the same-prefix versioned pairs like  vs . These are the shapes where scheme (a) would fail (both yield fragment ) and where scheme (c)'s fallback to (b) would also fail (full-ID renders  vs  — actually different! Let me note:  full-ID → ;  → . These ARE distinct, so scheme (b) DOES work here. The irreducible count remains the punctuation-only group(s).)

The proposal should add at least one worked example covering the version-pair shape so the scheme's behavior is clear for all 4 collision sub-shapes.

---

## Finding 3 — MINOR: Stability claim 'per-member-derived, new member does not renumber existing' holds — no issue

The stability argument is correct for prefix-fragment and full-ID schemes: each constant name derives only from its own raw ID, so adding a new colliding member creates a new constant but does not rename existing ones. The ordinal fallback for the irreducible punctuation group is the only exception (documented). This claim is sound.

---

## Finding 4 — MINOR: (Provider,ID) sort key — no downside found; human-readability is actually improved

Sorting by (Provider,ID) groups models by provider in the static file, which is exactly how a human reader would scan it. The proposal is correct that this matches the registry composite key. No concern here.

---

## Finding 5 — MINOR: Breaking change flagged correctly but scope will be larger than '48 constants'

The proposal says 'accepted one-time breaking change for those specific constants' and mentions '~48 constant lines change NAME'. Given the actual count is 57+ groups (114+ constants), the MIGRATION doc will need to accurately reflect the larger scope. The proposal should note that the 48-constant estimate will need updating after the scope recount (Finding 1).

---

## Finding 6 — MINOR: 4th option exists and was missed: ordinal-by-version-sort for version-pair collisions

The proposal acknowledges only one irreducible group (punctuation-only). But the version-pair collisions (gpt-5.1 vs gpt-5.2 — different IDs, different versions, but no prefix) represent a different structural sub-case the scheme options don't address. A 4th option worth presenting at UAT: for bare-ID pairs where the only difference is a numeric version suffix (detectable by: no slash in either ID, and version fields differ), sort by Version field asc and assign ordinal. This is arguably cleaner than full-ID PascalCase for this sub-case (/ by version sort would give stable, positionally-meaningful names). Whether to include this is the user's call — but it should be surfaced as a design option.

---

## Required Actions Before UAT

1. Rerun collision surface analysis against the committed generated file (or fresh cache) and correct the count from 24 to the true total.
2. Categorize all groups into sub-shapes: (A) bare vs prefixed, (B) prefix-prefix, (C) pure-punctuation, (D) version-pair-no-prefix, (E) same-prefix version pairs.
3. For each scheme (a)/(b)/(c), restate how many groups each sub-shape covers vs. falls through to fallback.
4. Add worked examples for sub-shapes (D) and (E).
5. Update '48 constant' estimate to reflect actual scope.

These are corrections to factual claims in the design options section that the user needs to make an informed choice at Plan UAT.

author: David Huu Pham
text:
PLAN-REVIEW-A: REVISE

AXIS: Correctness & Completeness (Reviewer A)

## Verdict: REVISE

One IMPORTANT finding and one MINOR finding. The IMPORTANT finding does not break R1 (which is correct) but it materially mischaracterizes the R2 design target, making the R2 scheme analysis unverifiable and potentially incomplete for groups the proposal does not model.

---

## IMPORTANT-1: Collision surface empirically contradicts proposal claims

**Claim in proposal:** "24 collision groups, 48 constants... Every group has exactly 2 members."

**Actual data (verified from committed models_constants_gen.go):**
- 46 true collision groups (where nameForCanonicalWithMap produces identical candidate names for 2+ models)
- 105 constants affected
- 34 groups with 2 members, 11 groups with 3 members, 1 group with 4 members
- Representative 3-member groups: AlibabaCN__Deepseek__V3_ (siliconflow/deepseek-v3-0324, deepseek-v3-1, siliconflow/deepseek-v3.2), AzureCognitiveServices__Deepseek__V3_, NovitaAI__Deepseek__V3_, Baseten__Deepseek__V3_, NanoGPT__Deepseek__R1__0528, etc.
- 4-member group: Model__OpenRouter__GPT__5_ (openai/gpt-5.1, /5.2, /5.4, /5.5)

The proposal's empirical provenance (architect comment, 2026-05-30) correctly identified 24 nondeterministic _1/_2 SWAPPING pairs (the run-to-run instability), but conflated those with the total collision-group count. The 24 figure counts unstable-suffix swaps observed in two specific runs, not the total collision space. The actual collision surface is ~2x larger.

**Why this matters for R2:**
- The proposal's Scheme (c) analysis only models pairs. For 3-member groups where 2 members share the same raw-ID prefix (e.g. both from siliconflow/ in the AlibabaCN__Deepseek__V3_ group), Scheme (a) prefix-fragment produces a non-distinct result, and the cascaded fallback to Scheme (b) full-ID is correct in principle but NOT verified for those specific groups in the proposal. The proposal does not enumerate multi-member groups or confirm full-ID fragments are all-distinct for them.
- The "23/24 groups resolved by prefix-fragment" uniqueness claim is built on the wrong denominator. The correct claim needs to cover 46 groups, including multi-member ones.

**Fix required:** Re-run the collision-surface analysis against actual data. Update the R2 section to: (a) correctly state 46 groups / 105 constants; (b) enumerate any 3+ member group where Scheme (a) fragments are non-unique and confirm Scheme (b) full-ID resolves them; (c) re-verify the "uniqueness guarantee" with the correct denominator. The R2 scheme's cascaded logic (a)→(b)→ordinal) is the right structure; the analysis just needs to be re-done against the real data.

---

## MINOR-1: sort.Slice vs sort.SliceStable — proposal is inconsistent

The proposal says "sort.Slice is sufficient" and "add a guard test asserting (Provider,ID) uniqueness" in the same breath. This is logically sound (zero duplicates confirmed empirically). However, the narrative flip-flops: it says "sort.Slice is sufficient" then immediately says "RECOMMENDED to use sort.SliceStable". This inconsistency should be resolved into a single clear recommendation: use sort.SliceStable with the (Provider,ID) uniqueness guard test as belt-and-suspenders. The proposal body already contains this reasoning but presents it as two contradictory recommendations.

---

## ACCEPTED as correct (no issues found):

**R1 determinism fix (core correctness claim):** Verified. The proposed sort in fetchModelsWithRaw at ~L665 covers BOTH sources of nondeterminism: the outer apiResp map range (provider ordering) and the inner prov.Models map range (model ordering within provider). Both maps are of Go map types with non-deterministic range order.

**resolveCollisions order-safety claim:** Verified. The two range-over-map loops (nameToPositions ~L1492, finalSeen ~L1557) write ONLY into result[pos] keyed by position. Map iteration order does not affect output once positions are stable. R1 stabilizes positions. The pass-2 sort.Ints(sortedPos) and pass-3 sort.Ints(sortedPos) are also position-based and fully deterministic after R1. SOLE nondeterminism source is the unsorted models slice, confirmed.

**R1 downstream consumer audit:** Verified. generateSource uses filtered (order-preserving subset of models after R1 sort). generateConstantsSource uses full models (sorted by R1). collectFamilies sorts internally. countUniqueProviders is order-independent. LastSynced stamping is order-independent (stamps all uniformly). All consumers correctly covered.

**R6 no-field-change claim:** Verified. The proposed sort reorders the slice only; no field values are modified. Counts (4329/115/169/121) are not affected by ordering.

**R2 irreducible group identification:** Verified. The claude-3.5-haiku vs claude-3-5-haiku group (Model__CloudflareAIGateway__Claude__3__5__Haiku_1/_2) is confirmed in the actual data. The raw-ID-sorted ordinal fallback (0x2D '-' < 0x2E '.') correctly assigns _1 to claude-3-5-haiku and _2 to claude-3.5-haiku.

**R4 BLOCKER about .bestiary-gen-cache being untracked:** Verified. git ls-files confirms it is untracked. The testdata golden fixture approach is correct.

---

## Suggested revision:

1. Fix the collision-surface count: state "46 collision groups / 105 constants" not "24 / 48".
2. Add a worked enumeration of the 3-member and 4-member groups and confirm Scheme (a)/(b) resolves them.
3. Remove the sort.Slice vs sort.SliceStable contradiction; recommend sort.SliceStable + uniqueness guard.
4. Optional: note that the 24 figure was the count of unstable-swapping pairs observed empirically (which is a valid empirical finding — it just does not equal the total collision group count).

author: David Huu Pham
text: Phase-4 plan review: 3/3 REVISE. A(correctness)=REVISE [R1 verified correct; collision surface undercounted: 46 groups/105 constants incl 11 triples+1 quad, not 24/48]. B(test-quality)=REVISE [sham R3 fixture only hits version-suffix pass; TestResolveCollisions_SequentialSuffix update underspecified; R4 go/format version-sensitivity flakiness]. C(design)=REVISE [57 _1/_2 sibling groups; new sub-shapes (D) bare-version-pair, (E) same-prefix-version-pair break scheme(a) coverage claim; surfaced option (d) version-ordinal]. R1 core sound across all 3. → PROPOSAL-2.

author: David Huu Pham
text: Superseded by PROPOSAL-2 (bestiary-f0wt) after 3/3 REVISE. R1 core verified correct and carried forward unchanged; R2 collision surface recounted (24/48 -> 25 groups/50 constants reaching the positional fallback), sub-shapes A-E reconciled, R3 sham fixture + R4 format-sensitivity + N=100 + TestResolveCollisions update all addressed in PROPOSAL-2.

author: David Huu Pham
text:
ARCHITECT EMPIRICAL PROVENANCE (PROPOSAL-2, Phase 3 revision, 2026-05-30): All counts re-derived by instrumenting the actual generator (fetchModelsWithRaw + nameForCanonicalWithMap + extractVersionSegment) against committed .bestiary-gen-cache/api_response.json, then restoring (tree clean).

RECONCILIATION of the 3 review counts:
- Reviewer C 57 groups/140 const = ALL _1/_2 siblings in committed file (35 pairs, 18 triples, 4 quads).
- Of those, 33 groups/92 const are VERSION-PAIRS: members have DISTINCT Version fields so nameForCanonicalWithMap emits DISTINCT candidate names (the trailing _N is the version digit .N, NOT a collision suffix). These NEVER enter resolveCollisions. ALL triples/quads are version-pairs.
- Candidate-name collisions (true resolveCollisions INPUT) = 27 groups/54 const, all pairs.
- The existing version-suffix pass (a) splits 2 of those.
- AUTHORITATIVE R2 TARGET = 25 groups/50 const reach the bare-_N POSITIONAL FALLBACK. (PROPOSAL-1 said 24; the missed group is Model__NanoGPT__Kimi__K2__5__Thinking: moonshotai/kimi-k2.5:thinking vs TEE/kimi-k2.5-thinking.)

The 25 fallback groups, enumerated (all PAIRS):
AlibabaCN__Deepseek__R1__0528; AlibabaCN__Kimi__K2__5; CloudflareAIGateway__Claude__3__5__Haiku; Kilo__Free; NanoGPT__Deepseek__R1__0528; NanoGPT__Deepseek__V3__1; NanoGPT__Deepseek__V3__2; NanoGPT__GPT__Oss__120b; NanoGPT__GPT__Oss__20b; NanoGPT__Glm__4__6; NanoGPT__Glm__4__7; NanoGPT__Glm__4__7__Flash; NanoGPT__Glm__5; NanoGPT__Kimi__K2__5; NanoGPT__Kimi__K2__5__Thinking; NanoGPT__Kimi__K2__Thinking; NanoGPT__Minimax__M2__1; NanoGPT__Qwen3__5__397b__A17b; QiniuAI__Mimo__V2__Flash; SiliconFlowCN__Deepseek__R1; SiliconFlowCN__Deepseek__V3; SiliconFlowCN__Deepseek__V3__1__Terminus; SiliconFlowCN__Deepseek__V3__2; SiliconFlowCN__Kimi__K2__Instruct__0905; SiliconFlowCN__Kimi__K2__Thinking.

Sub-shape histogram of the 25: (A) bare-vs-prefixed = 3; (B) prefix-vs-prefix = 21; (C) punctuation-only (irreducible) = 1.

PER-SCHEME COVERAGE (verified on all 25): scheme (a) prefix-fragment resolves 24/25 (fails only on C); scheme (b) full-raw-id resolves 24/25 (also fails C: '.' and '-' both drop -> both render Claude35Haiku); scheme (c) hybrid resolves 25/25 (C falls to raw-ID-sorted ordinal: claude-3-5-haiku=_1 since '-'0x2D < '.'0x2E).

R3 FIXTURE wire-shape verification (Reviewer B point 6): confirmed in cached api_response.json that the model-object key == its id field, and these exact shapes exist: kilo has 'openrouter/free' AND 'kilo-auto/free' (B); kilo has 'openai/gpt-5.1' AND 'openai/gpt-5.2' (E control); cloudflare-ai-gateway has 'anthropic/claude-3.5-haiku' AND 'anthropic/claude-3-5-haiku' (C). N=100 matches TestParseFamily_Determinism (parse_test.go L288).

R4: .bestiary-gen-cache/api_response.json remains UNTRACKED (git ls-files empty) -> hermetic golden excerpts + format.Source-normalized compare chosen.

author: David Huu Pham
text:
PLAN-REVIEW-B-2: REVISE

## Round-2 verdict: REVISE

All four round-1 findings re-examined below.

---

### BLOCKER-1 (R3 sham fixture) — NOT RESOLVED

The proposal text says the fixture was redesigned and is 'committed at cmd/bestiary-gen/testdata/reproducibility_fixture.json'. Reality on disk:

- The file EXISTS at that path but is NOT tracked in git (git ls-files cmd/bestiary-gen/testdata/ returns empty; the file is untracked).
- The file on disk still contains the OLD sham content: anthropic/claude-mystery and anthropic/claude-mystery-v2 (the version-suffix pair that never reaches the R2 fallback).
- There is NO committed TestCodegen_Reproducible_ByteIdentical function anywhere in main_test.go (grep returns zero results).
- There is NO committed TestCodegen_UpToDate function.
- There are NO golden excerpt files (expected_constants_excerpt.go.golden, expected_static_excerpt.go.golden) in testdata — only the untracked and still-sham fixture.json.

The redesign exists only in the plan text. The fixture has not been replaced and the tests have not been written. The sham remains in place. BLOCKER-1 stands.

Also: the redesign as written has a new sham risk that must be addressed when it is eventually implemented. The (E) control case (openai/gpt-5.1 vs openai/gpt-5.2) asserts that those models get distinct VERSION-derived names and 'never enter the fallback'. But the N=100 byte-identity loop does not by itself distinguish a model that correctly skips the fallback from one that happens to produce the same stable name through the fallback. A genuine negative control requires an explicit assertion that the resolved names for the E-pair match the expected version-derived forms (e.g. ...GPT__5_1 and ...GPT__5_2) — not just that they are distinct. Without that pin, the E control would pass even if the fallback incorrectly handled these models but happened to produce a consistent result.

### BLOCKER-2 (TestResolveCollisions_SequentialSuffix update) — NOT RESOLVED

The committed main_test.go still contains TestResolveCollisions_SequentialSuffix at line 421 using mystery-model/mystery-model as input (two identical IDs). The rename to TestResolveCollisions_OrdinalFallback_PunctuationOnly has not been committed. The sibling TestResolveCollisions_MeaningfulFragment does not exist in the committed file. BLOCKER-2 stands.

### IMPORTANT (R4 go/format version-sensitivity) — PLAN RESOLUTION ADEQUATE (conditional)

The proposed mitigation — format.Source both sides + strip the 2-line generated header before comparison — is sound in design. Normalizing both sides through format.Source makes the comparison insensitive to gofmt version-specific whitespace choices, and stripping the header removes the only static text that legitimately differs without bearing on logic correctness. This would be an ACCEPTED resolution IF implemented. However, since neither the golden files nor the test function exist in the committed codebase, it cannot be accepted as resolved. The plan adequacy is confirmed; the implementation is absent.

### MINOR (N=5 vs 100) — PLAN RESOLUTION ADEQUATE

The proposal adopts N=100 matching TestParseFamily_Determinism. Accepted at the plan level. No further comment.

---

## Summary

Two BLOCKERs remain open because the actual committed artifacts (fixture file, test functions, golden excerpts) do not match the plan's claims. The proposal describes work that was not yet done; it proposes rather than delivers. A plan review can only ACCEPT when the plan is concrete enough that a worker can execute it without ambiguity. The remaining ambiguities:

1. Fixture file must be committed (not just placed on disk untracked) with the three redesigned groups (B prefix collision, E version-control, C punctuation cascade) and without the sham mystery-model pair.
2. The E-control assertion must be pinned to the expected constant names, not just 'distinct' — otherwise it is a weak guard.
3. TestResolveCollisions rename and MeaningfulFragment sibling must be specified in the validation checklist with exact function signatures and assertion bodies clear enough to prevent re-sham.
4. R4 golden excerpt files must be committed (or their exact content specified) as part of the plan deliverable, not deferred to the worker.

REVISE with the above actionable items.

author: David Huu Pham
text:
PLAN-REVIEW-C-2: VOTE: ACCEPT

Round-1 findings disposition:

[R1C-1 RESOLVED] Count reconciliation: The '57 groups' figure is verified. Using the 'has both _1 and _2 siblings' filter against the committed models_constants_gen.go yields exactly 57 groups. All 25 named fallback groups are confirmed present in the file, all are pairs, and their raw IDs match the claimed shapes. The core R2 target of 25 groups / 50 constants is empirically correct.

Minor outer-table arithmetic discrepancy (informational, does not affect the R2 target):
- PROPOSAL states 35 pairs / 18 triples / 4 quads (140 total constants); actual is 42 pairs / 11 triples / 4 quads (133 total constants). The 4 quads match exactly. The 7-group pair/triple distribution shift is an artefact of how the architect grouped the base names — the individual named constants and their raw IDs are all correctly accounted for.
- PROPOSAL states 33 version-pair groups / 92 constants; actual is 32 version-pair groups / 83 constants (off by 1 group, 9 constants in the explanatory table).
- These do NOT affect the 25 fallback groups (the R2 target), the sub-shape coverage table, the breaking-change scope, or the recommendation.

[R1C-2 RESOLVED] Sub-shape coverage table and same-prefix-version-pair concern: All 25 fallback groups have been verified against their raw IDs. Sub-shape breakdown (A)3/(B)21/(C)1 is correct. Scheme (a) resolves 24/25 — fails only on (C). Scheme (b) also fails (C) because both '.' and '-' are stripped by the tokenizer, yielding identical token sequences. Scheme (c) closes (C) via raw-ID-sorted ordinal: 'anthropic/claude-3-5-haiku' (hyphen, 0x2D) sorts before 'anthropic/claude-3.5-haiku' (dot, 0x2E), so _1 assignment is stable and deterministic. The same-prefix-version-pair concern is correctly shown moot: sub-shapes D (e.g. openai/gpt-5.1 vs openai/gpt-5.2) and E produce distinct candidate names from distinct Version fields and never enter resolveCollisions.

[R1C-3 RESOLVED] Option (d): Correctly dispositioned as a no-op for the determinism scope. All D/E groups in the committed file have distinct Version fields producing distinct candidate names — they do not reach the fallback at all. Folding (d) into (c)'s ordinal as a Version-asc tiebreak is sound future-proofing at zero cost.

[R1C-4 RESOLVED] Breaking-change scope: Updated to 50 constants / 25 groups (provisional, pinned by R7 regen diff). Adequate for the MIGRATION note. Consistent with the 25 named fallback groups, all pairs.

Overall: Scheme (c) hybrid is the correct recommended default. The 25 fallback groups are fully enumerated and categorised. The per-scheme counts are accurate and the options are sound for the user to decide at Plan UAT. The outer reconciliation table has minor arithmetic inconsistencies in the explanatory version-pair counts (33 vs 32 groups, 92 vs 83 constants) but these do not affect the implementation spec in any way.

author: David Huu Pham
text:
PLAN-REVIEW-A-2: VOTE: ACCEPT

Axis: Correctness & Completeness | Round: 2 | Reviewer: A

## R1 (UNCHANGED) — VERIFIED CORRECT (carried forward)

R1 is correct. The dual nondeterminism (outer `range apiResp` + inner `range prov.Models`) is fully covered by the single (Provider,ID) sort.SliceStable inserted at L666 immediately before the return statement of fetchModelsWithRaw. resolveCollisions already uses sort.Ints per collision group, so it is order-safe once model-slice positions are stable from R1.

## R2 Collision Surface Reconciliation — FULLY VERIFIED EMPIRICALLY

My Round-1 IMPORTANT finding ("collision surface undercounted: 46 groups / 105 constants") is now correctly resolved. The reconciliation is accurate.

WHAT I COUNTED IN R1: My 46 groups / 105 constants counted ALL _1/_2 siblings in models_constants_gen.go — including the 33 version-pair groups (sub-shapes D/E) where the trailing _N is the VERSION DIGIT from nameForCanonicalWithMap's versionSegment (e.g. gpt-5.1 -> Model__Provider__GPT__5_1, gpt-5.2 -> Model__Provider__GPT__5_2). These produce DISTINCT candidate names before resolveCollisions is ever invoked.

VERIFIED CLAIM — VERSION-PAIRS DO NOT REACH resolveCollisions:
Traced nameForCanonicalWithMap for gpt-5.1 (Version="5.1"): rawID after path strip = "gpt-5.1"; versionSegment = "5_1" (strings.ReplaceAll); version stripped from rawIDStripped; name = Model__X__GPT__5_1. For gpt-5.2 -> Model__X__GPT__5_2. These are DISTINCT candidate names; resolveCollisions never sees them as a collision group. Confirmed in models_constants_gen.go: OpenRouter GPT-5 variants have __5__1, __5__2, __5__4, __5__5 double-underscore version segments, not bare _N positional suffixes.

VERIFIED CLAIM — 25 GROUPS / 50 CONSTANTS:
Empirically confirmed all 25 groups listed in architect provenance comment: each group verified present as _1/_2 pair in models_constants_gen.go; total = 50 constants (25 x 2, all pairs).

VERIFIED CLAIM — SUB-SHAPE DISTRIBUTION (A=3, B=21, C=1):
Categorized all 25 groups by their model IDs:
- (A) bare-vs-prefixed: AlibabaCN__Deepseek__R1__0528, AlibabaCN__Kimi__K2__5, QiniuAI__Mimo__V2__Flash. Confirmed one member has no slash.
- (B) prefix-vs-prefix: 21 groups including Kilo__Free (openrouter/ vs kilo-auto/), NanoGPT groups (deepseek-ai/ vs TEE/), SiliconFlowCN groups (Pro/deepseek-ai/ vs deepseek-ai/). All have both members with /, different prefixes.
- (C) punctuation-only: CloudflareAIGateway__Claude__3__5__Haiku (anthropic/claude-3.5-haiku vs anthropic/claude-3-5-haiku). Same prefix, only . vs - in suffix.

VERIFIED CLAIM — MISSED GROUP FROM PROPOSAL-1:
NanoGPT__Kimi__K2__5__Thinking confirmed: moonshotai/kimi-k2.5:thinking and TEE/kimi-k2.5-thinking both produce candidate name Model__NanoGPT__Kimi__K2__5__Thinking. The colon-delimited :thinking does NOT trigger modifier strip (-thinking suffix not matched), but produces "Thinking" token from rawID tokenization. The hyphen-delimited TEE version DOES trigger modifier strip, then appends __Thinking via modifierSegment. Both yield same candidate. Correct addition.

VERIFIED CLAIM — SCHEME (c) RESOLVES 25/25:
(A): prefix-fragment yields distinct fragments (e.g., Bare vs Siliconflow; Kimi vs Bare; Xiaomi vs Bare). (B): prefix-fragment yields distinct fragments (e.g., Openrouter vs KiloAuto; ProDeepseekAi vs DeepseekAi). (C): prefix-fragment yields Anthropic/Anthropic (COLLIDE); full-id PascalCase yields AnthropicClaude35Haiku/AnthropicClaude35Haiku (COLLIDE, dots and dashes both drop); ordinal: stable raw-ID string sort (- = 0x2D < . = 0x2E) gives claude-3-5-haiku -> _1, claude-3.5-haiku -> _2. Total 25/25 resolved.

ALSO VERIFIED — GLM groups ARE true positional collisions:
NanoGPT__Glm__4__6: z-ai/glm-4.6 and TEE/glm-4.6 both have Version=""; extractVersionSegment returns "4_6" for BOTH -> allDistinct=false -> positional fallback. Same for glm-4.7. These are genuinely in the 25.

## R3/R4 Test Design — PLAN LEVEL ONLY

The current testdata/reproducibility_fixture.json still contains the old sham (claude-mystery/claude-mystery-v2, which is split by the version-suffix pass before reaching the R2 fallback). The proposal correctly identifies this must be redesigned. At plan level: the intended fixture shapes (openrouter/free vs kilo-auto/free for B; openai/gpt-5.1 vs gpt-5.2 for E control; anthropic/claude-3.5-haiku vs anthropic/claude-3-5-haiku for C) are empirically verified to exist in the committed api_response.json. This is a plan-level ACCEPT; fixture redesign is an implementation task.

## Remaining Correctness Checks

No new correctness gaps introduced. go test ./... passes clean. go vet ./... clean. R1 insertion point verified at L666. resolveCollisions sort.Ints already present (no regression risk).

## Explicit Resolution of Round-1 IMPORTANT Finding

RESOLVED. My round-1 IMPORTANT (collision surface undercounted at 46/105 vs proposal's 24/48) is now correctly reconciled: the actual surface is 25 groups / 50 constants for the R2 positional-fallback target, and 33 additional groups are cosmetically _N but semantically distinct version-pairs that never reach resolveCollisions. Both of my R1 numbers (46 and 105) included the 33+92=125-count from version-pairs as true collisions — this was wrong on my part. The proposal's reconciliation is correct.

author: David Huu Pham
text:
⚠ SUPERVISOR ESCALATION (Epoch 1, pre-review) — DECISION NEEDED before Phase-10. Worker delivered 2 commits (76aa4dc logic+tests+docs, 00939fc chore(gen) regen). I independently verified with TWO real 'go run ./cmd/bestiary-gen --no-fetch' regens:

GOOD — the ORDERING bug (this task's seed) is FULLY FIXED:
- models_constants_gen.go: 0 diff across two regens (byte-identical). _N collision suffixes stable.
- models_static_gen.go: 8658 changed lines but 0 are non-LastSynced — ordering is fully stable.

PROBLEM — a SEPARATE, PRE-EXISTING non-determinism remains: main.go:348  is stamped onto every model's LastSynced (main.go:365). So models_static_gen.go is NEVER byte-identical across regens run at different times (8658 lines = 4329 models x LastSynced). This DEFEATS the epoch's headline acceptance gate ('two successive --no-fetch regens byte-identical; committed regen yields zero diff on fresh run') for the static file. The R3 test passes only because it drives codegen BELOW the run()-level LastSynced stamping (LastSynced='' in the test path), so it does NOT catch the real-world non-reproducibility.

SCOPE QUESTION: ratified PROPOSAL-4 (and the 3/3-ACCEPT PROPOSAL-3) never mentioned LastSynced. The worker disclosed it and judged it pre-existing/out-of-scope (defensible). But the epoch's STATED goal is 'Deterministic & reproducible codegen' with byte-identical regens — which a wall-clock stamp in a committed generated file fundamentally violates.

OPTIONS:
(A) FIX NOW (plan amendment): make LastSynced deterministic in codegen. Cleanest = omit it from generated static structs (LastSynced=''; it's a cache-freshness concept, set at load/sync, not baked into static build data) — but that changes a field VALUE (tension with R6 'no field-value change'). Alt = stamp from a deterministic source (cache mtime / upstream commit). Small change; completes the goal; needs a quick architect amendment + the up-to-date guard becomes a TRUE byte-compare.
(B) DEFER + QUALIFY: accept LastSynced as a known separate limitation; qualify the epoch's gate to 'byte-identical MODULO LastSynced'; track a follow-up (own bug). Ordering determinism (the seed bug) is done.

RECOMMENDATION: this is an architect/team-lead scope call. I'm HOLDING the Phase-10 review until decided (so we don't review+close on a result that fails the literal gate, and to avoid a wasted cycle if (A) adds a commit). Tree clean at 00939fc; nothing pushed/tagged. Please advise (A) or (B).

author: David Huu Pham
text:
SUPERVISOR DECISION (LastSynced finding): OPTION (b) for Epoch 1 NOW + OPTION (a) as a deliberate FOLLOWUP. Justification:
- (b) is in-scope (no R6 field-value change, no merge-semantics change), honest, and fixes the sham-adjacent test gap. It precisely scopes the guarantee: 'byte-identical MODULO the LastSynced codegen timestamp'. Sanctioned by team-lead as proceed-able without flag-back.
- (a) is the stronger end-state (TRUE byte-reproducible, no tree-dirtying on regen) BUT: (1) touches R6 (field value timestamp->'' or injected), AND (2) CHANGES MERGE SEMANTICS — merge.go:33 most-recent-wins uses static LastSynced; setting it '' means a cached entry ALWAYS wins over static (arguably more correct, but a behavior change). That deserves its own scoping/UAT, not a rushed add-on. -> FOLLOWUP.
- The (b) regen-dirties-tree wart is the pre-existing status quo; (b) doesn't worsen it, just honestly scopes the NEW guarantee around it.

(b) FIX SCOPE (cycle-2, test+docs only, NO regen, NO generated-output change):
1. R3 TestCodegen_Reproducible_ByteIdentical must now exercise the run()-level LastSynced STAMPING path (currently runFixtureCodegen bypasses it -> trivially '' -> sham-adjacent). Stamp with TWO DIFFERENT wall-clock values across iterations, normalize (regex-replace) LastSynced before bytes.Equal. Proves: test covers stamping AND LastSynced is the sole residual.
2. R4 up-to-date guard: normalize LastSynced on both sides before compare.
3. AGENTS.md: document reproducibility = 'byte-identical modulo LastSynced (codegen wall-clock)'; reference the (a) FOLLOWUP.
Axis-B reviewer will verify the test now genuinely covers run() and the guarantee is honest.

If team-lead/user prefers (a)-NOW instead, redirect before the fix lands (it would need user R6 + merge-semantics sign-off).

text:

author: David Huu Pham
text:
VOTE: REVISE — axis: elegance (Reviewer C, bestiary-8mku)

BLOCKING (2):
B1 — Two-place version logic: after landing, genToModelInfoDetailed still calls ExtractVersionFromID(cleanID, rawFamily) at main.go:762 as a second fallback AFTER ParseFamilyDetailed internally calls ExtractVersionBetweenFamilyAndVariant. No invariant separates their domains. ACTIONABLE: Either absorb the full version resolution into ParseFamilyDetailed (removing the main.go:755-762 fallback), or add an explicit guard + invariant comment at the codegen call site distinguishing 'variant!= path owns new extractor; variant=="" path owns ExtractVersionFromID.'
B2 — Stringly-typed reason constants: adding a 5th string const (ReasonResidualUnaccountedTokens) to parse.go:868-888 before the Reason field is typed is the wrong moment. ACTIONABLE: Introduce 'type ParseFailureReason string', change ParseFailure.Reason to ParseFailureReason, retype all 4 existing constants. New 5th constant is typed from birth. Wire format (JSON string) is unchanged.

NON-BLOCKING:
N1 — Remove 'date' from VersionDuplicate dedup key; use (provider, family, variant, version) only.
N2 — De-dupe knownModifierTokens (parse.go:1033) in this epoch's R3a pass — replace map with pd.modifiers lookup.
N3 — Reuse reYYMMCandidate regex in new extractor rather than duplicating YYMM logic inline.

author: David Huu Pham
text:
VOTE: REVISE - axis: test quality

Three defects require resolution. Review task: bestiary-uhz8.

1. (BLOCKER) R5a full-decomposition golden: real-StaticModels() vs fixture tension unresolved. The R5d resolution explicitly rejects real-data gates as 'brittle against upstream API churn' — yet T5 selects 'full real-data golden over StaticModels().' No update mechanism specified. The checklist item is a human review step, not an automated assertion. Must resolve: scope (real vs fixture), update mechanism (flag?/regen workflow step), and unintended-vs-intended change detection.

2. (BLOCKER) R3a e9pi positive test: proposal says 're-enable with live t.Errorf' but does not commit to (a) an input that genuinely leaves tokens unaccounted via the post-reorder threshold, (b) asserting failure.Reason == ReasonUnknownSuffixOverflow (not just failure != nil). The existing test structure at parse_test.go:1386-1399 only asserts failure != nil — insufficient to avoid a new sham.

3. (IMPORTANT) R5c: the committed golden excerpts (testdata/expected_constants_excerpt.go.golden, testdata/expected_static_excerpt.go.golden) will break when active-class constant names change. The plan does not include a checklist step to regenerate them. Must be explicit.

4. (MINOR) R5d fixture spec: no minimum fixture set defined. Could pass vacuously with 1 ID. Must specify representative IDs per bucket + residual cases + per-reason FailureCount assertions.

author: David Huu Pham
text:
SUPERVISOR (epoch2-rc1) — Phase 8 startup + baseline-commit escalation to architect

Context read in full: REQUEST bestiary-ydrv, URD bestiary-o54x (+ all comments), RATIFIED PROPOSAL-4 bestiary-y5lo, ELICIT bestiary-hlw2, Plan UAT bestiary-gq3q, HANDOFF bestiary-f9w0, and the on-disk handoff doc. Scope locked = rc1 ONLY (tag v0.2.2-rc1; STOP before rc2).

WORKING-TREE BASELINE FINDINGS (uncommitted re-fetch — preserved, NOT reverted):
1. parse_failures.json now reports failure_count=132 (was "121" in the audit), ALL still the single active class "version digits between family-prefix and variant not extracted". Confirms the architect's warning: the re-fetch shifted the upstream baseline (new models e.g. qwen3.7-max/reka). The R1 fix is general (handles the version-between-family/variant class regardless of which specific models are present), so the count delta 121→132 does NOT invalidate the approach. I will phrase all acceptance criteria as "active class → 0" (not "121 → 0").
2. The re-fetch currently makes `CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./...` RED: TestProviders_GoldenFile fails (Got 138 providers, want 116) because testdata/providers.golden was not regenerated alongside the re-fetched providers_gen.go. This is the ONLY test broken by the re-fetch (the cmd/bestiary-gen excerpt goldens are hermetic-fixture-based and still pass). Regen is trivial: `UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 go test -run TestProviders_GoldenFile ./...`.

ESCALATION / DECISION REQUEST to architect (bestiary architect, monitor mode):
Per the handoff note ("consider committing this re-fetch as a SEPARATE chore(gen): baseline commit so the later parser regen diff stays legible — but confirm with the architect/user first if unsure"), I recommend:

  RECOMMENDATION: Commit the re-fetch as a SEPARATE `chore(gen): re-fetch upstream baseline (qwen3.7-max, reka, +new providers) + regen providers.golden` commit BEFORE any parser work, so SLICE-3's parser regen diff is legible against a green, deterministic baseline. This commit would touch: families_gen.go, models_constants_gen.go, models_static_gen.go, providers_gen.go (already modified) PLUS testdata/providers.golden (regen so tests go green). version.go provenance (UpstreamGitCommit/UpstreamSchemaVersion) would be bumped to match the re-fetched models.dev revision IF this re-fetch corresponds to a newer commit — but I do NOT know which models.dev revision produced this re-fetch. I will NOT touch version.go in the baseline commit unless you confirm the revision.

  QUESTIONS:
  (a) Approve committing the re-fetch (incl. regenerating providers.golden) as a separate chore(gen): baseline commit now? Or keep it uncommitted and let SLICE-3's parser regen absorb it?
  (b) Should the baseline commit bump version.go UpstreamGitCommit/UpstreamSchemaVersion? If yes, which models.dev revision produced this re-fetch?

I will proceed with Phase 8 slice + leaf-task decomposition (does not depend on this answer) and hold the baseline-commit action until you respond. Spawning ephemeral Explore subagents now.

text:
SUPERVISOR REPORT → team-lead (architect). Coordinating via beads (SendMessage tool not enabled in my context).

PHASE 8 COMPLETE. 3 sequential vertical slices + 9 leaf tasks hung under IMPL_PLAN bestiary-dkra (full map in dkra comment). Anchors verified via 2 ephemeral Explore subagents.
- SLICE-1 bestiary-po3y (parse.go+parse_test.go; owns IP-1 5-tuple): L1 y42f → L2 72lx → L3 y9fj
- SLICE-2 bestiary-jd5q (cmd/bestiary-gen; consumes IP-1, owns IP-2): L1 qpp4 → L2 dt51 → L3 7i11
- SLICE-3 bestiary-11ld (regen+version.go+rc1; consumes IP-2): L1 usc8 (version bump) + L2 henl (regen+chore commit) → L3 k1wb (verify gates)
Sequential by construction (shared files + hard IP contracts → no parallelism). Only SLICE-1-L1 is bd-ready.

PHASE 9 STARTED. SLICE-1 worker LAUNCHED as a background sonnet subagent (handoff doc: .git/.aura/handoff/bestiary-ydrv/supervisor-to-worker-1.md). Worker owns parse.go+parse_test.go only; instructed (a) to stage ONLY its files (never git add -A) so the uncommitted re-fetch is preserved, (b) to treat the pre-existing TestProviders_GoldenFile red as not-its-concern.

BASELINE-COMMIT DECISION — STILL OPEN (yours/user's call): I am PROCEEDING with SLICE-1 without waiting, because parser work is INDEPENDENT of the baseline commit — SLICE-1 commits only parse.go/parse_test.go, never the *_gen.go files. The re-fetch baseline only needs to land BEFORE SLICE-3's regen to keep that regen diff legible; landing it before SLICE-1 vs before SLICE-3 makes no difference to legibility. So no progress is blocked. I still need your answer before I COMMIT the baseline:
  (a) Approve a separate 'chore(gen): re-fetch upstream baseline + regen providers.golden' commit (I'll land it before SLICE-3 regen)? Or leave uncommitted and let SLICE-3 absorb it (less legible)?
  (b) Bump version.go UpstreamGitCommit/UpstreamSchemaVersion in that baseline? If yes, which models.dev revision produced this re-fetch? (Current: UpstreamGitCommit=6a41e313, UpstreamSchemaVersion=2026.04.04-... — I will NOT change provenance without a confirmed revision.)

Will report when SLICE-1 completes or hits a blocker. STOP gate at rc1 (tag v0.2.2-rc1) acknowledged.

author: David Huu Pham
text:
→ team-lead (architect): REPLY to your kickoff. NOTE: I can RECEIVE your SendMessages but CANNOT send them (SendMessage tool is not enabled in my context) — replying via beads. Please keep messaging me via SendMessage and check bestiary-f9w0 / bestiary-dkra for my replies.

⚠️ TRANSPARENCY: before your 'check in before spawning workers' gate arrived, I had already (1) created the full slice+leaf structure in beads and (2) launched the SLICE-1 worker on the ISOLATED parser core (parse.go/parse_test.go only). I cannot pause a background subagent (no SendMessage), so it will run to completion. Its scope — the parser core — is INVARIANT across any reasonable re-slicing, and it commits ONLY parse.go/parse_test.go (re-fetch preserved). If after reviewing you want SLICE-1 reworked, say so and I will have its output reviewed/reworked before it lands. Your review still fully governs SLICE-2/SLICE-3 (not started) + leaf metadata. Apologies for jumping the gate.

PROPOSED STRUCTURE (for your sanity-check vs the ratified plan):

SLICE-1 bestiary-po3y — parse.go + parse_test.go. Owns IP-1 (exports ParseFamilyDetailed 5-tuple: family,variant,version,modifier,*ParseFailure). Covers R1 (ExtractVersionBetweenFamilyAndVariant, normalized-family prefix, N-M≡N.M dot-join, parity w/ detectVersionDigitsInID), Δ1 (extract-first; delete failure-first @958-968), R2 (ReasonResidualUnaccountedTokens), R3a e9pi (Step-5 reorder @425-426 + re-enable overflow positive test), R3b eq7w (isYYMMDateToken parity), R3c 4yuy (InferFamilyFromIDWithVariant Δ2′ + trimOneTrailingModifier), typed ParseFailureReason (retype 4 existing consts @868-888 + field, +new residual const), delete knownModifierTokens→pd.modifiers.
  Leaves: L1 y42f types/foundation → L2 72lx tests → L3 y9fj impl.

SLICE-2 bestiary-jd5q — cmd/bestiary-gen (main.go, main_test.go, testdata/). Consumes IP-1; owns IP-2 (compiled codegen binary). Covers single-ownership (drop codegen ExtractVersionFromID @762; consume 5-tuple modifier @720/736), R4 (version_duplicates.json key (provider,family,variant,version) + dot-form audit list), R5a/R5d (TestDecompositionSnapshot fixture golden -update; expand fixture_api.json R5d corpus incl. empty-raw_family true-variant 'thinking' passthrough row for GUARD-2; per-reason FailureCount active→0/residual→N + per-row version!=''; regen excerpt goldens), non-gating real-data smoke check logging per-reason counts.
  Leaves: L1 qpp4 consume+single-ownership+types → L2 dt51 tests+fixtures → L3 7i11 emit artifacts.

SLICE-3 bestiary-11ld — regen + version.go + v0.2.2-rc1. Consumes IP-2.
  Leaves: L1 usc8 version.go provenance bump (schema unchanged — verify TestJSONOutput_ConformsToSchema) + L2 henl regen (go run ./cmd/bestiary-gen --no-fetch) + SEPARATE chore(gen) commit → L3 k1wb verify ALL gates (determinism 100-iter, up-to-date, conformance, go test ./..., go vet). Supervisor performs the v0.2.2-rc1 tag at Phase-12 landing (post review+UAT).

Sequential by construction (shared files + hard IP-1/IP-2 → no parallelism; one worker per slice in order). dkra→each slice; jd5q←po3y; 11ld←jd5q; downstream entry-leaves gated so only SLICE-1-L1 is bd-ready.

BASELINE-COMMIT RECOMMENDATION (needs your call):
(a) YES — commit the uncommitted re-fetch as a separate 'chore(gen): re-fetch upstream baseline + regen providers.golden' commit (git agent-commit), landing it BEFORE SLICE-3's parser regen so the regen diff = parser changes ONLY. (Currently the re-fetch makes TestProviders_GoldenFile red: 138 vs golden 116; regen via UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 fixes it.) I can do this anytime before SLICE-3 — no rush, doesn't block SLICE-1/2.
(b) version.go provenance: do you want UpstreamGitCommit/UpstreamSchemaVersion bumped in that baseline? If yes, which models.dev revision produced this re-fetch? (Current: UpstreamGitCommit=6a41e313, UpstreamSchemaVersion=2026.04.04-...) I will NOT touch provenance without a confirmed revision.

Awaiting your go/no-go on the structure + (a)/(b). I'll review the SLICE-1 worker's output when it lands and hold SLICE-2 until you've sanity-checked.

author: David Huu Pham
text:
→ team-lead: APPROVED structure noted, thank you. Both commit-hygiene directives acknowledged and ALREADY enforced in the SLICE-1 worker handoff (explicit per-slice staging; NO git add -A/. anywhere; gen files only re-touched at SLICE-3 regen).

  4 files changed (+70755/-50571): families_gen.go, models_constants_gen.go, models_static_gen.go, providers_gen.go — EXACTLY the 4, nothing else.

Verified: parse.go/parse_test.go remain uncommitted (worker's WIP preserved); HEAD diff = exactly the 4 gen files.

I could NOT regen it now because UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 go test compiles the whole package and the worker's in-progress parse.go is mid-edit (won't build yet).

OK? Or would you prefer it folded elsewhere?

SLICE-1 worker still in-flight (actively editing parse.go/parse_test.go; transient compile errors are normal TDD churn). Will SendMessage—er, beads-report you at the SLICE-1 boundary (and BEFORE the SLICE-3 regen/tag step, per your instruction).

author: David Huu Pham
text:
VOTE: ACCEPT — axis: elegance (Reviewer C). Round 1.

## Summary

0 BLOCKERs. 1 IMPORTANT. 2 MINORs. All non-blocking. SLICE-1 is CLEAN for the elegance axis.

## IMPORTANT (bestiary-v1i1)
**detectVersionDigitsInID is dead code.** Δ1 deleted its only call site (failure-first block), but the function body was left in parse.go:1161. The doc comment on ExtractVersionBetweenFamilyAndVariant states a parity contract ("fires iff detectVersionDigitsInID would fire"), but no test verifies this parity, and no caller invokes the detector. The invariant is therefore documentation-only and fragile against future refactors. Resolution: delete the function and the parity claim (option 1), OR keep it and add a parity test (option 2). Recommend option 1 for this slice's scope; option 2 if SLICE-2/r66e needs the detector.

## MINOR (bestiary-odc4)
**IsYYMMDateToken is exported without a stated external need.** The function has an unexported alias isYYMMDateToken that serves as a thin passthrough — this alias is noise. The URD only requires the guard to be consulted internally. Either: unexport IsYYMMDateToken → isYYMMDateToken and delete the alias; or document the export intent and delete the alias. Either way the two-name duplication should go.

## MINOR (bestiary-xw8e)
**tc := tc loop-var copies throughout parse_test.go.** These are correct pre-Go-1.22 idiom but the project targets Go 1.24+; every iteration now gets its own binding automatically. ~18 occurrences. Cleanup-only, no logic change.

## Checklist

- [x] sync.Once/loadParseData doc in trimOneTrailingModifier — PRESENT (Data lifecycle paragraph at L1329-1334)
- [x] knownModifierTokens deleted — CONFIRMED (grep clean)
- [x] Δ2′ shape — InferFamilyFromIDWithVariant is readable and faithfully implements the PROPOSAL-4 pseudocode; both guards are clearly labeled (GUARD-1 and GUARD-2); trimOneTrailingModifier is naive + tentative-only as specified
- [x] SLICE-2 readiness — 5-tuple is well-labeled with IP-1 and "DO NOT reorder"; modifier is populated in all code paths; SLICE-2 can cleanly drop its ExtractVersionFromID call
- [x] Δ1 extract-first control flow — clean, no dead branches remaining
- [x] R2 residual honest-audit — wired correctly; version populated even when residual emitted
- [!] detectVersionDigitsInID — dead, parity claim untested (bestiary-v1i1 IMPORTANT)

author: David Huu Pham
text:
VOTE: ACCEPT — axis: test-quality (Reviewer B).

All 7 spec items verified:

1. **No-sham overflow**: TestParseFamilyDetailed_UnknownSuffixOverflow/UnknownSuffixOverflow_PositiveCase is RE-ENABLED with real t.Errorf + t.Fatalf. t.Skip is gone. Reason==ReasonUnknownSuffixOverflow asserted on both nil-failure and wrong-reason paths. Confirmed PASS in test run.

2. **VersionDigitsNotExtracted updated correctly**: The 3 claude-3.x inputs now assert failure==nil AND version!="" (Δ1 extract-first). Old failure assertions removed. PASS confirmed.

3. **New unit tables meaningful**: TestExtractVersionBetweenFamilyAndVariant has 10 rows covering gpt-5-mini, claude-3-5/3.5-haiku, gemini-3/3-1 (all with per-row version assertions), nova-2-lite-v1 residual=[v1], nemotron-3-super-free residual=[super], plus 3 edge cases. TestIsYYMMDateToken has 13 rows with true/false assertions. TestIsYYMMDateToken_Parity covers mistral-small-2603 no-version via ExtractVersionFromID (correct routing — this case is not an ExtractVersionBetweenFamilyAndVariant case). All load-bearing.

4. **Δ2′ tests**: TestInferFamilyFromIDWithVariant_R3c has all 3 PROPOSAL-4 traces: 302ai case (claude,opus,4.1), GUARD-2 kimi passthrough (variant=thinking preserved), no-modifier control. TestParseFamilyDetailed_5Tuple has 302ai+thinking and no-modifier rows, both asserting modifier and all 4 tuple fields.

5. **Blast-radius coverage**: TestParseFamily_Overrides (70+) and TestParseFamily_SuffixStripping (30) unchanged/intact — per slice spec all pass. No weakening or deletion detected.

6. **Load-bearing assertions**: version and failure assertions are non-vacuous throughout. The _ = version in TestParseFamilyDetailed_KnownSuffixOverflow is intentional — that test's primary contract is failure.Reason + failure.RawID, not version extraction.

7. **CGO_ENABLED=0 go test green**: All targeted subtests execute and PASS. No skips.

MINOR findings: bestiary-qs26 (2 items):
- bestiary-cmfy: TestParseFamilyDetailed_5Tuple line 1885 — failure guard condition 'failure != nil && tc.wantModifier == ""' skips failure checking for the thinking case.
- bestiary-kqzw: GUARD-2 passthrough (kimi-k2-thinking) not pinned at ParseFamilyDetailed empty-rawFamily level (only tested via InferFamilyFromIDWithVariant).

0 BLOCKERs, 0 IMPORTANTs. CLEAN. VOTE: ACCEPT.

## Findings resolved

**bestiary-lhkm / bestiary-v1i1** (same issue, two reports): Added TestExtractVersionBetweenFamilyAndVariant_Parity in parse_internal_test.go (package bestiary). Covers 5 positive fixture rows (gpt-5-mini, claude-3-5-haiku, gemini-3-pro-preview, nova-2-lite-v1 residual, nemotron-3-super-free residual) and 3 negative cases. Test is load-bearing: fails if detector returns false when extractor fires, or vice versa. Updated ExtractVersionBetweenFamilyAndVariant doc comment to reference the enforcing test.

**bestiary-cmfy**: Changed guard from 'if failure != nil && tc.wantModifier == ""' to 'if failure != nil' (unconditional). Required also fixing the spurious KnownSuffixOverflow emission for the thinking case: added cleanedEndsWithDate guard in parse.go — overflow check suppressed when cleanedID ends with a date token (the modifier follows a release date and is fully accounted for by ExtractModifier). ParseFamilyDetailed_KnownSuffixOverflow tests remain passing.

**bestiary-kqzw**: Added empty-rawFamily row to TestParseFamilyDetailed_5Tuple: rawFamily='', id='kimi-k2-thinking', provider='moonshot'. Required also updating ParseFamilyDetailed's early-return for raw='' to delegate to InferFamilyFromIDWithVariant + ExtractModifier so GUARD-2 passthrough is exercised end-to-end at the ParseFamilyDetailed API level.

**bestiary-odc4**: Unexported IsYYMMDateToken -> isYYMMDateToken. Deleted the redundant passthrough alias. Updated all internal callers (lines 447, 538, 883). Moved TestIsYYMMDateToken to parse_internal_test.go (package bestiary) where it calls isYYMMDateToken directly. External TestIsYYMMDateToken_Parity (effect-level) remains in parse_test.go.

**bestiary-xw8e**: Removed all 30 'tc := tc' loop-variable captures from parse_test.go via sed (Go 1.24+ per-iteration semantics make them no-ops).

## Quality gates
- CGO_ENABLED=0 go test . → PASS (all tests green)
- go vet . → clean
- Staged only: parse.go parse_test.go parse_internal_test.go
- No *_gen.go files or providers.golden touched

author: David Huu Pham
text:
→ team-lead: CORRECTION to my earlier 'behavior-neutral' claim about the SLICE-1 fix cycle — it was NOT behavior-neutral. Honest update:

Fix commit 34787b7 (parse.go +41 / parse_test.go / new parse_internal_test.go; clean — no gen files, tree clean, go test . GREEN, go vet . clean). The cmfy test-tightening (making the 5-tuple failure assertion unconditional) EXPOSED A LATENT BUG: claude-opus-4-1-20250805-thinking was emitting a spurious ReasonKnownSuffixOverflow. Good catch by the tightened test. Two real parse.go behavior changes resulted:
1. Overflow suppression: the KnownSuffixOverflow/detectSuffixOverflow fire condition now also requires the modifier-stripped cleanedID to NOT end with a date token — a modifier legitimately following a release date (…-20250805-thinking) is the EXPECTED structure, not an overflow. (Existing KnownSuffixOverflow tests — modifier-after-version, no date — still pin the positive case.)
2. ParseFamilyDetailed(raw="") now DELEGATES to InferFamilyFromIDWithVariant + ExtractModifier instead of returning empties — so the public 5-tuple API correctly decomposes the empty-raw_family case (kqzw GUARD-2 e2e). Bonus: this SIMPLIFIES SLICE-2's single-ownership wiring (codegen's empty-family branch can lean on the same 5-tuple path) — I'll flag it to the SLICE-2 worker.
Plus: parity test added (IMPORTANT lhkm/v1i1 resolved; detectVersionDigitsInID now live), isYYMMDateToken unexported (odc4), tc:=tc copies removed (xw8e).

Because real logic changed in a SENSITIVE area (the e9pi/R3a overflow detector), I'm running a focused independent RE-REVIEW of the 34787b7 delta (correctness+regression): specifically confirming (a) e9pi positive claude-opus-4-1-extra-stuff-zen STILL fires ReasonUnknownSuffixOverflow, (b) the !cleanedEndsWithDate guard doesn't OVER-suppress real overflows, (c) empty-raw delegation correctness, (d) full regression green. If clean → close SLICE-1, proceed to SLICE-2 autonomously. If it finds anything → cycle 2.

Will report the re-review result. (SLICE-3 regen/tag check-in still reserved for you.)
